model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: UCLA holds a meaningful ~6.6-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over UCF (KenPom #27 vs. #54, Torvik #24 vs. #58), with an elite offense ranked 22nd nationally that is well-positioned to exploit UCF's below-average defense ranked 114th by Torvik. The Bruins' most significant health concern was resolved when leading scorer Tyler Bilodeau (mild right knee strain) was cleared to play in the NCAA Tournament, restoring UCLA to near full strength after he exited the Big Ten Tournament quarterfinal win over Michigan State. UCF brings genuine Big 12 credentials — five Quad 1 wins and a 21-11 record — and their offense (39th nationally) gives them a credible scoring path, but their defensive deficiencies and the 5.5-point market spread reflect a consistent edge for the Bruins that is difficult to overcome in a single-elimination setting.

reasoningStep.title: UCLA vs. UCF — Round of 64 East Region (7 vs. 10)
reasoningStep.summary: UCLA is the clear but not overwhelming favorite, backed by a ~6.6-point efficiency gap, elite offense, Bilodeau's return from injury, and Big Ten battle-testing. UCF has legitimate Big 12 credentials and a capable offense but a vulnerable defense that UCLA's top-22 attack should exploit. The 5.5-point spread and ~68% win probability align with a confident but not certain UCLA pick.
evidence: UCLA KenPom #27 (AdjEM +21.64), Torvik #24; UCF KenPom #54 (AdjEM +15.05), Torvik #58 — ~6.6-point efficiency gap
UCLA AdjO ranked 22nd nationally (123.8 KenPom, 124.5 Torvik) vs. UCF AdjD ranked 114th (105.7 Torvik) — major offensive matchup advantage for UCLA
Tyler Bilodeau (UCLA leading scorer) cleared to play in NCAA Tournament after mild right knee strain — key health concern resolved
UCLA opened as 5.5-point favorite, O/U 153 — market confirms meaningful but not dominant edge
UCF has 5 Quad 1 wins (Kansas, Texas Tech, others) and a 21-11 Big 12 record — legitimate résumé, credible upset threat
UCF beat Cincinnati in OT in Big 12 Tournament before losing to #2 Arizona 81-59 — competitive but not elite
Historical 7-over-10 upset rate ~40%, keeping this a genuinely contested matchup despite UCLA's edge

- UCF: KenPom #54 (AdjEM +15.05), Torvik #58 | AdjO: 120.5/120.0, AdjD: 105.5/105.7 — good offense, below-average defense
- **Efficiency gap: ~6.6 points (KenPom), ~7 points (Torvik)** — meaningful but not enormous

**Injury news:** UCLA's leading scorer Tyler Bilodeau (knee strain) was **cleared to play** in the NCAA Tournament — major positive development for UCLA

**UCF context:** 21-11 record in the Big 12, five Quad 1 wins (Kansas, Texas Tech, etc.), beat Cincinnati in OT in Big 12 Tournament before losing to #2 Arizona 81-59. Legitimate résumé but defense ranks 114th (Torvik) — a real vulnerability

**UCLA context:** 23-11 in the Big Ten, top-22 offense nationally, beat Michigan State in Big Ten Tournament quarterfinals (without Bilodeau in the second half). Battle-tested Big Ten schedule

**UCF upset path:** UCF's offense (39th nationally) can score, and 7/10 upsets happen at a ~40% historical rate. But their defense (114th) is a liability against UCLA's elite offense, and Bilodeau's return removes the key uncertainty that could have flipped this game
